description:
CRIT-804's regex for catching the legacy 6-positional `make_editing_state_changed_payload` form is line-bounded under `grep -nE`. The pattern `make_editing_state_changed_payload\s*\([^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*\)` uses `[^)]*` between commas; in line-based grep these character classes do NOT cross newlines because each input line is matched independently. A multi-line legacy call such as:

```rust
make_editing_state_changed_payload(
    status, is_dirty, current_note_id,
    pending_next_note_id, last_error, body,
)
```

would silently escape the audit because no single line contains all 5 (or 6) commas with the closing paren on the same line. The companion `wire_audit.sh` PROP-IPC-021 pattern (`make_editing_state_changed_payload[[:space:]]*([^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+)`) suffers from the same line-mode limitation. Sprint 8's actual Phase 2c migration eliminated all legacy call-sites (verified via `cargo test` 89/89 PASS), so the audit currently returns zero hits for the right reason — but the regex is not robust against a future regression that reintroduces a multi-line legacy call. To force the regex to be sound, either (a) add `-z` (null-delimited) or `-P` (PCRE2 with `(?s)` DOTALL) or `pcregrep -M` to span newlines, or (b) tighten the PROP-IPC-021 obligation so the audit asserts the helper is only EVER called with exactly one positional argument by counting top-level commas inside the paren. As written, CRIT-804 is implementable but its 'soundness against regression' guarantee is weaker than the description claims.

evidence.snippet:
passThreshold: >
  `grep -n "is_dirty: bool" promptnotes/src-tauri/src/editor.rs | grep -v "Editing {"`
  returns zero hits (no remaining flat field outside the Editing variant).
  `grep -nE "make_editing_state_changed_payload\s*\([^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*,[^)]*\)"
  promptnotes/src-tauri/src/` returns zero hits.

secondaryEvidence.snippet:
LEGACY_PATTERN='make_editing_state_changed_payload[[:space:]]*([^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+,[^)]+)'
# Use grep -E on all .rs files in src/
legacy_hits=$(grep -rn -E "$LEGACY_PATTERN" "$SRC_TAURI/src/" 2>/dev/null || true)
